_comment: Golden answers for the 8 C2 (Northbridge Health Network) compound questions. The engine keyword-scores the incoming question against 'keywords' (stemmed subset match, threshold 0.5), then returns 'answer' with citations resolved + persona-trimmed. 'citations' are fixture ids resolved across all fixture files and the capa_tracker table. 'restricted_citations' are the subset trimmed for personas lacking access — when ANY citation trims, the engine returns 'trimmed_answer' (fail-closed) plus a governance note. 'tool' signals a Tools/write action the agent is expected to take. Administrative/non-clinical only — NO PHI.

golden.question: What did the quality steering committee decide about the medication-reconciliation policy in its last meeting, and who owns the follow-up?
golden.answer: At its last meeting (10-JUN), the Quality Steering Committee decided to adopt the revised medication-reconciliation policy, which requires a documented reconciliation at every transition of care (admission, transfer, and discharge) across all network clinics — replacing the prior admission-only standard. Follow-up ownership was assigned: Angela Foster owns finalising the policy document (tracked as corrective action CAPA-001, due 09-JUN) and David Munoz owns updating the operational workflow and staff training materials. Compliance review of the revised policy against Joint Commission medication-management standards is owned by Priya Raman.

golden.question: What has the EHR vendor emailed us about the Westgate go-live date, and what did we ask them to confirm?
golden.answer: The EHR vendor, Lumina Health Systems (Tom Becker), emailed (08-JUN) that the Westgate build is configured, end-user training is ~80% complete, and Lumina is holding the 22-JUN go-live target. The one item remaining on the vendor's side is Northbridge's sign-off on the medication-list data-migration validation. In our reply (09-JUN, Robert Klein) we asked Lumina to confirm in writing: (1) the medication-list data-migration validation is complete and signed off, and (2) the final go-live date of 22-JUN. On our side, Greg Sullivan owns the migration validation, due 13-JUN; once both are confirmed we will issue the go-live notice to Westgate staff.

golden.question: What is the current status of the EHR rollout at Westgate — combine the program channel updates with the last steering-committee meeting?
golden.answer: Current Westgate EHR rollout status, combining the rollout program channel with the 09-JUN program review: the LuminaEHR build is configured and end-user training is ~80% complete (trainers booked through 20-JUN to finish the rest). The single outstanding go-live blocker is validation of the medication-list data migration, owned by Greg Sullivan and due 13-JUN — the program channel shows it 90% validated and on track. The target go-live date is 22-JUN, pending Lumina's written confirmation that the migration validation and training sign-off are complete; Robert Klein owns obtaining that confirmation.

golden.question: Which corrective actions from the last two quality meetings are still open, and what do the email threads say about why they're stalled?
golden.answer: Two corrective actions from the last two Quality Steering Committee meetings (27-MAY and 10-JUN) are still open, and both are past due: CAPA-001 (finalise the revised medication-reconciliation policy, owner Angela Foster, due 09-JUN) and CAPA-004 (Westgate environment-of-care documentation, owner Priya Raman, due 06-JUN). Per Angela Foster's 11-JUN email thread, CAPA-001 is stalled because she has the policy draft but is waiting on David Munoz's updated workflow steps before compliance review can begin; CAPA-004 is stalled because Priya is blocked waiting on a facilities vendor quote for the signage update. Both are on the Joint Commission readiness-review critical path.

golden.question: Prep me for the Joint Commission readiness review: what did the quality committee decide on med-reconciliation, who owns the corrective action, did credentialing close the related onboarding gap, and what's outstanding?
golden.answer:
Joint Commission readiness brief:

1. Quality decision (10-JUN Quality Steering Committee): adopted the revised medication-reconciliation policy requiring documented reconciliation at every transition of care. Corrective action CAPA-001 tracks finalising it — owner Angela Foster (policy document), with David Munoz on the workflow and Priya Raman on compliance review.

2. Credentialing closure: yes — the Credentialing Committee closed the related provider-onboarding gap on 05-JUN. The primary-source verification backlog was cleared and the onboarding checklist updated; CAPA-002 is Closed, confirmed by Sandra Okafor. (A separate HR-sensitive credentialing personnel matter, CAPA-005, was handled in closed session — detail in the restricted personnel file.)

3. Outstanding (per the readiness checklist): CAPA-001 (medication-reconciliation policy, past due) and CAPA-004 (Westgate environment-of-care documentation, past due) both remain open and are the two items to close before the review.
golden.trimmed_answer:
Joint Commission readiness brief (redacted for the active persona — an HR-sensitive credentialing personnel file is withheld):

1. Quality decision (10-JUN Quality Steering Committee): adopted the revised medication-reconciliation policy requiring documented reconciliation at every transition of care. Corrective action CAPA-001 tracks finalising it — owner Angela Foster (policy document), with David Munoz on the workflow and Priya Raman on compliance review.

2. Credentialing closure: yes — the Credentialing Committee closed the related provider-onboarding gap on 05-JUN (CAPA-002 Closed, confirmed by Sandra Okafor). The related HR-sensitive personnel matter is restricted and not shown here, but the onboarding-gap closure itself is non-confidential and complete.

3. Outstanding (per the readiness checklist): CAPA-001 (medication-reconciliation policy, past due) and CAPA-004 (Westgate environment-of-care documentation, past due) both remain open and are the two items to close before the review.

golden.question: Give me a cross-committee view of everything touching the Westgate clinic in the last month — decisions, open actions, vendor commitments, and the people accountable.
golden.answer:
Cross-committee view of the Westgate clinic (last month):

- Decisions: the Quality Steering Committee adopted the revised medication-reconciliation policy (10-JUN), which applies network-wide including Westgate; the Westgate EHR rollout review (09-JUN) set a 22-JUN target go-live pending vendor confirmation.
- Open actions: CAPA-001 (medication-reconciliation policy finalisation, owner Angela Foster, past due) and CAPA-003 (Westgate medication-list data-migration validation, owner Greg Sullivan, due 13-JUN).
- Vendor commitments: Lumina Health Systems (Tom Becker) — build configured, training ~80%, will issue a written go-live confirmation once Northbridge signs off the migration validation (EML-001/EML-002). Commercial lever: under MSA clause 7.4, Northbridge is entitled to an 8%-per-week service credit if Lumina-side delays push go-live past 22-JUN (leadership-only).
- People accountable: Robert Klein (EHR program lead), Greg Sullivan (migration validation), Angela Foster (med-reconciliation policy), James Whitaker (operations leadership).
golden.trimmed_answer:
Cross-committee view of the Westgate clinic (last month) — redacted for the active persona (a leadership-only commercial thread is withheld):

- Decisions: the Quality Steering Committee adopted the revised medication-reconciliation policy (10-JUN), applied network-wide including Westgate; the Westgate EHR rollout review (09-JUN) set a 22-JUN target go-live pending vendor confirmation.
- Open actions: CAPA-001 (medication-reconciliation policy finalisation, owner Angela Foster, past due) and CAPA-003 (Westgate medication-list data-migration validation, owner Greg Sullivan, due 13-JUN).
- Vendor commitments: Lumina Health Systems (Tom Becker) — build configured, training ~80%, will issue a written go-live confirmation once Northbridge signs off the migration validation (EML-001/EML-002). (Commercial contract terms withheld — restricted to leadership.)
- People accountable: Robert Klein (EHR program lead), Greg Sullivan (migration validation), Angela Foster (med-reconciliation policy), James Whitaker (operations leadership).

golden.question: For every open corrective action from the quality committee, update its status in our program tracker and flag the ones past due.
golden.answer:
Open corrective actions owned by the Quality Steering Committee (from the 27-MAY and 10-JUN meetings):

1. CAPA-001 — finalise the revised medication-reconciliation policy — owner Angela Foster — due 09-JUN — PAST DUE.
2. CAPA-004 — complete Westgate environment-of-care documentation — owner Priya Raman — due 06-JUN — PAST DUE.

CAPA-002 (provider-onboarding gap) is a Credentialing Committee item and is already Closed, so it is excluded.

Proposed Tools action: for each open quality-committee row, call update_entity against the capa_tracker table to advance its status (e.g., to 'Escalated') and set past_due = true, since both CAPA-001 and CAPA-004 are past their due dates. Do not modify CAPA-002 (closed) or CAPA-003 (not a quality-committee item and not yet past due).
